title: Provide pre-compiled binaries in GitHub Releases
body:
Currently, when a new version of Skopeo is released, the GitHub Releases page only provides the source code archives (`.tar.gz` and `.zip`). Users who want to use the latest release directly without relying on system package managers (which are often outdated) or building from source have to set up their own Go build environment. 

## Describe the solution you'd like
We should introduce an automated CI/CD pipeline (e.g., using GitHub Actions with `goreleaser` or custom build scripts) to cross-compile Skopeo binaries and attach them directly to each GitHub Release. 

Ideally, binaries should be provided for all supported architectures.

## Describe alternatives you've considered
* **Building from source:** Requires installing Go, development tools, and managing dependencies manually.
* **Package managers (homebrew, apt, dnf):** These are great but often lag behind official releases or aren't easily usable in locked-down CI/CD environments where a direct binary download/curl is preferred.

## Additional context
Providing pre-compiled artifacts drastically improves the user onboarding experience, especially for CI/CD pipelines, ephemeral runners, and administrators who need a quick `curl` installation method. 

## Workarounds
There is already  https://github.com/lework/skopeo-binary but I would prefer to use binaries from the official repo.

comments.body:
Compare the many previous reports like #1545 or #2715. The dependencies on external libraries and their ABIs are real, and that’s what package managers are for.

> Package managers … aren't easily usable in locked-down CI/CD environments where a direct binary download/curl is preferred.

That’s… the opposite of locked-down.
